extracted.headquarters: One Glenlake Parkway, 14th Floor, Atlanta, Georgia 30328
extracted.brandBackground: ServiceMaster Restore traces its roots to professional cleaning services begun in 1929; the current franchisor entity was formed in October 2020 following a securitization transaction and has offered Restore Franchises since March 2021, operating a network of approximately 1,910 disaster restoration franchises across the U.S.
extracted.conceptRationale: Franchisees provide disaster restoration services (water mitigation, fire/smoke cleanup, mold remediation, structural drying) to residential and commercial customers — a skilled trade service delivered on-site.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 requires full-time owner/manager involvement, at least one dedicated salesperson, and field technicians to deliver 24/7 emergency restoration services.

leadership.name: Jon Nobis
leadership.background: CEO since September 2024; previously Chief Information Officer for SM Manager (2021–2024) and CEO of Two Men and a Truck/International (2018–2022).
leadership.whyItMatters: Top executive accountable for overall franchise system strategy and performance.

leadership.name: John Tovar
leadership.background: Group President since March 2023, overseeing ServiceMaster Clean and Restore; previously President of ServiceMaster Restore (2020–2023).
leadership.whyItMatters: Direct operational leader for the Restore brand.

leadership.name: Sunil Doshi
leadership.background: CFO & Treasurer since August 2024; previously EVP/CFO of Fossil Group (2021–2024).
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees financial health and reporting for the franchisor entity.

leadership.name: Austin Yoder
leadership.background: COO since March 2025; previously President North America of ServiceMaster Clean and VP Franchise Operations of ServiceMaster Restore.
leadership.whyItMatters: Responsible for day-to-day franchise operations and franchisee support.

leadership.name: Josh Burnette
leadership.role: General Counsel & Chief Human Resources Officer
leadership.background: General Counsel since July 2023 and CHRO since June 2024; previously General Counsel North America for DS Smith Plc.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages legal risk, compliance, and HR across the SM franchise system.

leadership.name: Jeff Todd
leadership.background: Chief Franchise Development Officer since September 2025; previously Chief Development Officer at Launch Franchising and VP Development at Driven Brands.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise sales and network growth strategy.

item19.notes: Item 19 discloses gross service sales only — no EBITDA, net income, or profitability table is provided. Data covers Fiscal Year 2025 (ended December 31, 2025). Figures do not reflect costs of sales or operating expenses. No company-owned units operated in FY2025.

lineItems.notes: Non-refundable upon signing; includes AOS Training for two and Restore 365 Plus setup.
Truck mount, extractors, air movers, dehumidifiers, and other required restoration equipment.
Down payment and first month's payment on a 20' box truck (estimated $40,000–$80,000 total cost).
Laptop, high-speed internet, and one month of service.

lineItems.category: Travel and Other Expenses During AOS Training
lineItems.notes: Travel and living expenses for one to two people attending 12-day AOS Training in Memphis, TN.
Down payment on annual premiums for required coverage; varies by market and service offerings.
IICRC testing fees (WRT, ASD, FSRT, HST); low assumes already certified.
Minimum $10,000 required pre-opening Eligible Local Marketing spend including grand opening event.
Background checks, security/utility deposits, contractor's license, business licenses.
First and last month's rent for a Dispatch Office (3,500–5,000 sq ft); varies by market.
Legal and accounting fees for entity formation, lease review, and FDD review.
Working capital for payroll, rent, vehicle payments, advertising, and other operating costs for first 3 months assuming 3 employees.

flatMonthlyFees.name: Annual Convention Fee
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. 32 — currently $1,000 per attendee annually

flatMonthlyFees.name: Construction Services Fee
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. 35 — 3% of monthly Gross Service Sales from Construction Services

hiddenCosts.name: Restore 365 Plus Audit Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $15 per insurance claim audited through the Validate component of the software.

hiddenCosts.name: Xactimate Software License
hiddenCosts.description: $174.21 per month to license Xactimate estimating software from Xactware.

hiddenCosts.name: Cotality Subscription
hiddenCosts.description: $135–$209 per month for Cotality subscription to submit invoices and documents to insurance carriers.

hiddenCosts.name: Cotality Mitigate Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $7 per insurance claim documented through Mitigate software.

hiddenCosts.name: Claims Workspace Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $15 per job processed through Cotality's Claims Workspace platform.

hiddenCosts.name: XactAnalysis Processing Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $6.13–$36.98 per job processed through Xactware's XactAnalysis platform.

hiddenCosts.name: Business Intelligence Software Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $450 per quarter for designated business intelligence software.

hiddenCosts.name: Time and Materials Estimating Software
hiddenCosts.description: $1,099 per year for designated time and materials estimating software.

hiddenCosts.name: Optional Sketching Software
hiddenCosts.description: $95 per month plus $5–$45 per job for optional sketching program.

hiddenCosts.name: Optional Weather Forecasting Software
hiddenCosts.description: $417 per month for optional weather forecasting program.

hiddenCosts.name: Credentialing and Background Check Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Currently $165 per year for credential management and background check services for employees.

hiddenCosts.name: Optional Program Referral Fee – Commercial Jobs
hiddenCosts.description: Currently 5% of total invoiced amount per commercial job referred through Optional Programs.

hiddenCosts.name: Optional Program Referral Fee – Residential Jobs
hiddenCosts.description: Currently $125 per residential job referred through Optional Programs.

hiddenCosts.name: Estimating as a Service Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $125–$325 per water damage job (≤$50,000) or up to 5% of invoice for larger or other damage jobs.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $1,000 per single violation per day (up to $2,000) for failure to comply with Franchise Agreement or Manual standards.

hiddenCosts.name: Liquidated Damages
hiddenCosts.description: Average monthly royalties/fees over prior 12 months multiplied by the lesser of remaining term or 24 months, due within 7 days of termination.

hiddenCosts.name: AOS Training – Additional/Replacement Trainees
hiddenCosts.description: Currently $6,310 per person (up to $8,110) for additional or replacement trainees beyond the two included in the Initial Franchise Fee.

hiddenCosts.name: Ongoing Training and Regional Meeting Fees
hiddenCosts.description: $300–$2,500 per attendee for training programs; $1,000 per attendee for regional meetings.

hiddenCosts.name: ContentsTrack Inventory Software Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $31.84 per job for Xactware's ContentsTrack inventory management software.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty and Advertising Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay minimum royalty and advertising fund payments regardless of sales levels; failure may result in franchise termination and loss of investment.

operationalRisks.title: Minimum Monthly Sales Performance Requirement
operationalRisks.description: Beginning in month 13, franchisees must achieve at least $7,500 in monthly Gross Service Sales; failure three times in any nine-month period can result in termination.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution (Georgia)
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be mediated, arbitrated, or litigated in Atlanta, Georgia, which may increase costs and reduce settlement leverage for out-of-state franchisees.

operationalRisks.title: Non-Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees receive no exclusive territory and may face competition from other franchisees, company-owned units, or other channels within their designated area.

operationalRisks.title: Shrinking System Scale
operationalRisks.description: The network has declined from 2,071 to 1,910 units over three years (2023–2025), indicating net unit contraction that may affect brand support and resale value.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Right to Modify Business Model
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may unilaterally modify the Manual and system standards, potentially requiring additional investments or operational changes without franchisee consent.
operationalRisks.source: Item 17, p. 76; Item 3 (general franchising risks), p. 3

operationalRisks.title: Significant Litigation Settlement
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor settled a franchisee lawsuit in January 2026 for $1,795,000 involving claims of improper termination and territory violations, indicating legal exposure.

operationalRisks.title: Post-Termination Non-Compete
operationalRisks.description: Two-year non-compete within the Territory, a 25-mile radius, and a 5-mile radius of any customer location served in the prior 24 months restricts post-exit business options.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Sales Visit Requirement
operationalRisks.description: Salespeople must make at least 50 in-person or videoconference visits per month per 300,000 territory residents; failure three times in nine months can trigger termination.

operationalRisks.title: High Initial Investment with No Franchisor Financing
operationalRisks.description: Total initial investment of $287,800–$474,340 with no direct or indirect financing offered by the franchisor or its affiliates.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p. 44 — Real Estate Expenses estimated at $8,000–$25,000 representing first and last month's rent for a Dispatch Office of 3,500–5,000 sq ft; no per-sqft rate disclosed.

riskReasons: 3 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $381,070 mid-point build-out, there is a $131,070 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptRationale: Franchisees provide disaster restoration services (water mitigation, fire/smoke cleanup, mold remediation, structural drying) to residential and commercial customers — a skilled trade service delivered on-site.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing; positive net worth; the business is profitable; the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP): Net income of $68 on $367 of revenue — roughly 0.8x spent per $1 earned. Positive net worth of $761. Cash of $68 against $42 of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 3.65).

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. ServiceMaster Systems LLC guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
